blocks.content: In High Gear, the famous female entity known as the Operator is referred to as 'the Lady' among the administrators.
The first reason is that she never loses her elegance, even in the midst of a battlefield filled with firepower…

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: The Lady is upset again.

Don't get me wrong. You're the only oddball among the administrators who plays High Gear, so I asked. Are you okay with Yahweh2? If left alone, he might eventually topple the Operator's stronghold. Wouldn't it be better to expel him now?

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: What does that have to do with anything? The reason Operator is ranked number one has nothing to do with me being an administrator.
Number Seven chuckled.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: No, I was just worried you might get anxious. How about it? If you want, I can tweak the events a bit.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: Number Seven.
Her voice was cold.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: Don't get ahead of yourself. While twelve administrators built High Gear, I am the original designer. I cannot allow event expansions that go against the initial concept.
That sounds a bit annoying.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: I'll admit you're a genius, but we're here to experiment with a perfect world, not to create a playground for a lonely girl.
Screeeeech!
A high-pitched noise, loud enough to burst eardrums, struck the video in the administrator's meeting room.

blocks.speaker: The Operator
blocks.content: Do you want to die? What do you know about me? Should I blow your brain up right now?

blocks.speaker: The Operator
blocks.content: Try it if you can.
Number Seven! You…!
At that moment, the Lollipop mark shouted.

blocks.speaker: The Lollipop
blocks.content: Stop! Stop it!
Whether it would work or not, the skill to immediately block the Operator's noise attack was excellent.

blocks.speaker: The Lollipop
blocks.content: Every time we have a meeting like this, I can't even go to school. Can't we just focus on the agenda?

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: That guy keeps pissing me off!
And what about you? What are you going to do if an administrator gets too close to a user? Do you know how many times I have to bug test every time I prepare an event? It's nauseating.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: Look at it technically, technically. Just log in occasionally and see if everything's running smoothly. You're a god. You don't have to hunt all day. Just max out your status and sweep everything. I don't understand your idea of a perfect world anymore. What do you want to do in High Gear?
The airwaves became solemn.

blocks.speaker: The Operator
blocks.content: …I don't know.
The Operator spoke.

blocks.speaker: The Operator
blocks.content: Can a created world really be perfect? If users ignore it, it's false in the end.
Silence flowed through the thoughts.

blocks.speaker: The Operator
blocks.content: Yes, I ran away because I hated reality. So, I have no choice. What I want to do in High Gear is… probably just to live. My heart is here. So, for me, this place is real.
Number Seven asked.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: Are you satisfied with that? You made it all. What if a serious bug occurs? Can you live in High Gear if it becomes a mess?
The answer came late.

blocks.speaker: The Operator
blocks.content: …I just said I want to live. That's all. I'm done talking. I'll leave first. I have no personal objections to the agenda. Consider it when voting.
The High Gear mark disappeared.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: Hmph! She keeps her raid time sharp. Hey, I'm leaving too. Abstain on the agenda.
As Number Seven disconnected, the Smile mark quickly proceeded with the meeting.

blocks.speaker: Smile
blocks.content: Let's vote right away. Press 1 if you think Yahweh2 should be sanctioned, and 2 if you think there's no issue.
Numbers appeared on the ten screens.

blocks.speaker: Smile
blocks.content: Alright. I'll record today's meeting and lock it. See you at the next meeting.
Yahweh2, no issue.
Electronic Wasteland.
Shirone and his group hunted Metal Wolves with High Gear's output set to maximum.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hey! It's running away! Shoot! Shoot!
As the rifles fired, the Metal Wolf sharply changed direction.
Seeing only fragments flying from the innocent ground, Destroyer707 frowned.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Ugh, this is frustrating! Isn't it strange? Didn't we catch these easily before?
In fact, the average level of Metal Wolves was between 6 and 7, making them weak creatures.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: It's because our levels are too low. Usually, we level up to 7 in the Iron Homeland before coming out.
Shirone suggested a method.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We can't win with output alone. It's open terrain, so we can't use the geography. Let's create it ourselves.
Dig a trap?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No. One person shoots to extend the Metal Wolf's path. Then, two people run in a straight line to offset the speed difference. After driving it into a narrow path, the last person finishes it off.
Strongest Codename nodded.

blocks.speaker: Strongest Codename
blocks.content: Good idea. If we switch roles, we can level up evenly. Once we reach level 5, we should be able to catch Metal Wolves easily.
Following Shirone's strategy, the hunt resumed.
Screech! Screech!
As the Metal Wolf spread out on the ground with a sharp mechanical sound, their levels quickly increased.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This is great! Since our levels are so low, just catching one makes the experience gauge shoot up.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: Yeah. We'll level up in no time.
After hunting in turns, Shirone and his group all reached level 5.
Shirone's eyes sparkled.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A new function appeared. Search radius 200 meters.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: Yes. It's an AI function activated at level 5. Now, information about users or creatures within 200 meters will appear. The same goes for codenames.
It doubled. It was 100 meters at first.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: Wow, how did you know? It's not even in the manual. We found out by measuring it ourselves.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Just a mage's intuition, I guess.

The search radius is really important. Outside the search range, the AI doesn't work, so there's no auto-aim or anything. The only thing you can see is the codename, and that's within visible range. If you're 500 meters or 1 kilometer away, you can't even tell who attacked you.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: It's a useful feature for snipers. That's why Colonoscopy shot from exactly 200 meters.
That's basic. As the level goes up, the combat range exceeds at least 1 kilometer. Users also fly and shoot anti-personnel missiles.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I get it. As you level up, the AI's functions increase and improve. Plus, the output gets stronger, so even a one-level difference is huge.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: Exactly. There are five main types of AI. Right now, you've activated auto-aim, recoil correction, and search radius. At levels 7 and 9, you'll unlock one more each. I'll explain as we hunt. From level 10, you'll enter the world of selective options.
Ah, that's why it hasn't opened yet.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Honestly, it's fun. The system is designed to teach you step by step as you level up.
In that sense, the existence of the Tenmen was precarious.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Well, that's the operator's domain.
For now, Shirone, who wanted to enjoy High Gear as a user, went back to hunting.
Catching level 10 Metal Scorpions and Metal Worms made the experience points soar. The Scorpions shot acid from their tails, and the Worms' ability to hide underground was annoying.
At level 7, the penetration correction function was activated.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: That's good.
Death Princess explained.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: It's not a core function, but many variables arise from this penetration correction. The most important thing is that it's not about probability. Once it hits, it will penetrate the armor. So, no matter how strong the enemy is, there's a chance for a headshot.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: Really? Then can I beat Operator?
To get to the point, it's possible. But I think the probability is less than 0.001 percent.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: Let me explain. Aim your rifle at me.
When Shirone aimed at Death Princess's forehead, an aim appeared.
A 100% symbol appeared inside the aim, and a red line circled around it.
When the red line formed a perfect circle, the 100% symbol glowed gold.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah…

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: Got it? The important thing in penetration judgment is the AI's calculation time. Now, with an old helmet, the calculation took about 2 seconds, but if I had parts equipped, the time would increase significantly depending on the armor's durability. Of course, if you equip better firearms, the time will decrease.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I understand. So, what happens if I aim at Operator now? His durability must be the best. Wouldn't it take hours for the penetration judgment to come out?

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: No. In that case, instead of 100 percent, a 'critical' judgment will appear. The rifle's power limit can't exceed the armor's durability.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But you said 0.001 percent.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: That's if you equip better firearms. Anyway, Golden Wheel will support you. Do you want to fight with a rifle? And do you think Operator will wait until you activate penetration correction?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Well…

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: But it's still a really good function. You and Operator are extreme cases, but usually, if successful, it can penetrate armor ten times stronger than the weapon's power.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But it's that difficult.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: Yes, it is. So, it's more important in boss creature strategies. This is called a raid. At higher difficulties, the creature's durability becomes enormous. In such cases, a 'critical' judgment is like rain in a drought. If even one critical fails, some creatures are physically impossible to defeat.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yeah, I get it.
The hunt resumed.
From now on, the experience required to level up was so much that they had to hunt for three hours straight.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Let's stop for today. It's dark, and hunting efficiency drops. We need to conserve High Gear's power.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Power drops?

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Not something to worry about at this level, but just in case. Of course, at higher levels, the battery consumption is enormous.
Strongest Codename brought back some dry wood, which was rare in the wasteland.
Let's light a fire. We don't need a watch, but prey might come.
Using the flint they brought from the Iron Homeland, they lit a campfire, and Shirone sat by the fire.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The heat feels real.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Like a dream, perhaps.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yahweh2.
When he looked up, Death Princess was hesitating with a slightly flushed face.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: Um… we're going to have some fun. Do you want to join us?
Understanding what she meant, Shirone looked at Destroyer707 and Strongest Codename.
And from their calm demeanor, he felt it.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Right. This is…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A virtual world.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Their appearances must all be fake. Yeah, I'm probably the only one here who cares about feelings.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone politely declined.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Sorry. I'm a bit uncomfortable.
Alright, then.
Smiling, Death Princess returned to her friends and started a silent game.
Crackle. Crackle.
As the campfire's sparks flew wildly, Shirone fell into endless thought.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What is real?
The three people sitting across were kissing, and beyond them was a vast darkness.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Where is the heart…
Shirone reached out so close that he almost got burned.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Where is it?
The campfire was hot, and also very cold.
